reasoning: Taylor Swift's 'The Life of a Showgirl' debuted at #1 with a massive 4 million units but has exhibited a sharp standard decay, dropping to 338k in week 2 and 194k in week 3. By comparison, her previous album 'The Tortured Poets Department' had stronger holdovers (253k in week 3) and still only managed 12 consecutive weeks at #1. A 17-consecutive-week run at #1 has not been achieved in the modern era of the Billboard 200, as it requires fending off all competing major releases, surprise drops, and seasonal surges for essentially a full four months. Given the album's steep 95% unit decline by week 3 and the impending holiday season, reaching 17 straight weeks at #1 is extremely improbable. I am setting the probability to the minimum allowed bounded value of 0.05.
